skill.name: wechat-management
skill.description: Manage WeChat chat context and draft messages through the WeChatMCP server with confirmation checks before sending.
skill.summary: Use WeChatMCP to inspect chat state, draft replies, and send confirmed WeChat messages.
skill.author: 7Sageer, Claude

security_audit.summary: Static shell execution, weak cryptography, and entropy findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, front matter, and Chinese text. The remaining risk is medium because the skill can inspect WeChat state, use web search, and send messages through WeChatMCP, which can expose private chat context if misused.

medium_findings.title: WeChat MCP Message Access and Sending Risk
medium_findings.description: The skill relies on WeChatMCP to view chat state and send WeChat messages. Wrong recipient selection or missing confirmation could expose private information.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes WeChat management and screenshot-based state checks. It also includes confirmation rules, which reduce but do not remove privacy and mis-send risk.

medium_findings.title: Web Search May Expose Chat Context
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to use web search for real-time or uncertain information. Sensitive chat details could be sent to search if prompts are not minimized.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The web search instruction is explicit, but the file does not require sending private data. The risk depends on how the assistant forms search queries.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown formatting around MCP server and tool names. No executable command or shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap text such as WeChatMCP and get_screenshot in Markdown. There is no Ruby code, shell syntax, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography blocker appears to match text in the Markdown description. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API is used in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 only contains a natural-language description and a Markdown-formatted server name. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Markdown Content
low_findings.description: The entropy alert is likely caused by mixed English, Chinese text, and front matter. The file is readable Markdown, not binary or encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is plain text with visible headings, instructions, and examples. I did not find obfuscated payloads or encoded executable content.

low_findings.title: Low Risk: Example External URL in Reply Text
low_findings.description: A b23.tv link appears in an example message. It is not fetched by the skill file, but it shows the skill may include external links in outgoing replies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is plainly visible inside a reply example, not hidden in code. It may be benign, but external links in messages still need user review.

content.user_title: Manage WeChat Replies With Confirmed Sends
content.value_statement: WeChat conversations can require fast replies while keeping recipient and tone correct. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to inspect context, draft short replies, and confirm before sending.
actual_capabilities: Checks current WeChat state through the WeChatMCP screenshot tool.
Drafts short WeChat replies using the conversation style rules in the skill.
Adapts tone for familiar contacts, work groups, and unknown contacts.
Requires confirmation of the recipient and message content before sending.
Prompts for web search when real-time information or uncertain facts are involved.
Escalates tool failures by asking the user for the current situation.
limitations: Requires an available and trusted WeChatMCP server.
Does not provide independent WeChat access without MCP tools.
Cannot guarantee message delivery after an external tool failure.
May need extra review for sensitive, financial, or private information.

use_cases.title: Reply to Personal Chats
use_cases.description: Draft short, natural replies that match recent chat style and avoid overlong messages.

use_cases.title: Handle Work Group Messages
use_cases.description: Prepare concise professional responses for team groups while reducing casual emoji use.

use_cases.title: Verify Current Facts Before Replies
use_cases.description: Use web search before answering about news, products, or unfamiliar terms.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Simple Reply
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one chat response.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the current WeChat chat with WeChatMCP. Draft a short reply and ask me to confirm before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Match the Chat Tone
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the visible conversation style. Suggest two short replies that match the contact type and explain which one is safer.

prompt_templates.title: Reply With Fact Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: If the message includes current news or product facts, verify them with web search before drafting a WeChat response.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Sensitive Message Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with sensitive or financial content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the recipient and draft. If sensitive or financial details appear, ask for second confirmation before any send action.

output_examples.input: Ask my colleague if the meeting moved to 3 PM.
output_examples.output: Suggested reply: “会议改到下午3点了吗？” The assistant asks you to confirm the recipient before sending.

output_examples.input: Reply to a friend about a funny video link.
output_examples.output: The assistant drafts two short casual messages and keeps the link separate for easier review.

output_examples.input: Answer a group question about a new product release.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies current information first, then drafts a concise professional group reply.

best_practices: Confirm the recipient and final text before every send action.
Remove private chat details from web search queries whenever possible.
Use short messages and split complex replies into two or three parts.
anti_patterns: Do not send messages to contacts that were not confirmed by the user.
Do not browse public accounts or unrelated chats for extra context.
Do not include sensitive or financial details without second confirmation.

faq.question: Does this skill work without WeChatMCP?
faq.answer: No. It depends on the WeChatMCP server and its available tools.

faq.question: Can it send messages automatically?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the assistant to confirm the recipient and content before sending.

faq.question: Does it read all WeChat content?
faq.answer: No. The instructions say not to view public accounts or unrelated non-chat information.

faq.question: How does it handle sensitive information?
faq.answer: It requires second confirmation for sensitive or financial content.

faq.question: Why does it use web search?
faq.answer: It uses web search when current facts, news, products, or unfamiliar terms need verification.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing this skill?
faq.answer: Review the WeChatMCP permissions, message confirmation flow, and privacy handling for searched chat context.
